purpose: This project is a Next.js starter that demonstrates how to integrate Prisma ORM with Prisma Accelerate. It showcases various caching strategies for database operations using a simple Quotes management interface.
architectureSummary: The application uses the Next.js App Router to serve a frontend interface and API routes, communicating with a database via Prisma ORM configured for Accelerate. Components for rendering data are located in the components folder, while data access logic is managed through Prisma and shared utility functions.

techStack.name: Next.js
techStack.role: Serves as the core framework for the application, handling routing and server-side logic.

techStack.name: React
techStack.role: Provides the component-based library for building the user interface.

techStack.name: Prisma
techStack.role: Functions as the ORM to manage database interactions and data modeling.

techStack.name: Tailwind CSS
techStack.role: Handles styling the application layout and components.

techStack.name: TypeScript
techStack.role: Enforces type safety across the entire application codebase.

folderExplanations.explanation: Contains the application's page and API routing logic defined by the Next.js App Router.
Houses reusable UI components, specifically focusing on the Quote management features.
Stores shared utilities, type definitions, and helper functions used throughout the project.
Contains the database schema definitions and migration configurations.

entryPointNotes.note: Acts as the root layout for the Next.js App Router, defining the global structure of the application.
Serves as the home page of the application, acting as the primary entry point for user interaction.
